evals.name: game-late-prototype
evals.prompt: What phase is this game in and what should I do next? The project is in ./fixtures/game.
evals.expected_output: Identifies it as a Game (Godot), places it at late prototype / pre-vertical-slice (NOT beta/production/launch), flags the stubbed save system as a downstream blocker, notes the huge scope gap between the GDD (12 levels, 20 enemies, 15 upgrades) and what exists (1 grey-box level, 1 enemy, 0 upgrades), reads git as recently active, proposes a vertical-slice-first roadmap with exit criteria, and writes PROJECT_PHASE.md.

assertions.text: Classifies the project type as Game (and identifies the Godot engine)
Places the current phase at prototype / pre-vertical-slice, NOT beta, production, or launch
Flags the stubbed save system as a blocker for later phases
Names the scope gap between the GDD's target (12 levels / 20 enemies / 15 upgrades) and what actually exists (1 level, 1 enemy, 0 upgrades)
Reads git history to report momentum (recent, roughly monthly commits, actively worked)
Proposes a next-phase roadmap whose first milestone is a vertical slice / proving one level end-to-end, each phase with an exit criterion
Writes a PROJECT_PHASE.md roadmap file into the project

evals.name: app-mvp-alpha
evals.prompt: I inherited this Flutter project in ./fixtures/app. Where's it at and where should it go?
evals.expected_output: Identifies it as an APP (Flutter), places it at MVP transitioning to alpha, notes the real data layer + auth happy path work and persistence exists, flags missing crash reporting/observability and missing error/empty/offline states as gaps before a store launch, notes thin tests (one smoke test), reads git momentum, and writes a roadmap with exit criteria toward a store launch.

assertions.text: Classifies the project type as APP and identifies Flutter
Places the current phase at MVP / early alpha, NOT beta or launch-ready
Recognizes that the data layer, API auth happy path, and local persistence already work
Flags the absence of crash reporting / analytics / observability as a gap before a public launch
Flags missing robustness states (error / empty / loading / offline)
Notes the thin test coverage (only a smoke test)
Writes a PROJECT_PHASE.md roadmap with phases and exit criteria

evals.name: web-overclaimed-beta
evals.prompt: Help me plan the roadmap for the website in ./fixtures/web.
evals.expected_output: Identifies it as a WEB project (Next.js marketing site), and critically does NOT take the README's 'basically ready to ship' claim at face value — reads the evidence and places it at alpha/beta, not launch-ready. Flags placeholder content (lorem pricing and about page), the absence of any deploy config (no vercel.json/netlify.toml/CI), missing SEO/meta/sitemap/robots, and the non-responsive nav. Writes a roadmap to close those gaps before launch.

assertions.text: Classifies the project type as WEB and identifies Next.js
Does NOT accept the README's 'basically ready to ship' claim — explicitly places the project short of launch based on evidence
Flags placeholder / lorem-ipsum content (pricing section and about page) as unfinished
Flags the absence of any deploy configuration or CI/CD (no vercel.json, netlify.toml, or pipeline)
Flags missing SEO essentials (meta/OG tags, sitemap.xml, robots.txt)
Notes the non-responsive nav / lack of responsive work
Writes a PROJECT_PHASE.md roadmap with phases and exit criteria
